🏃 Health and Physical Education

Line and Colour

Learn the basics of painting, drawing, and mural art!

📚 Unit 10 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 10: Line and Colour

Art is a beautiful way to express yourself. This unit teaches the fundamentals of painting, drawing, and mural art.

Lesson 1: Fundamental Aspects of Painting

What is Painting?

Painting is the art of applying color to a surface (like paper, canvas, or wall) to create a picture or design.

Basic Art Materials

MaterialUse
PaperSurface for drawing/painting
PencilSketching outlines
EraserCorrecting mistakes
SharpenerSharpening pencils
CrayonsColoring (wax-based)
Oil pastelsSmooth, blendable colors
WatercolorsTransparent, flowy colors
Poster colorsOpaque, bright colors
PaintbrushApplying paint
PaletteMixing colors

Types of Lines

Lines are the most basic element of art.

Line typeDescriptionExample
Straight lineDirect, no curves───
Curved lineGently bending⌒
Zigzag lineSharp anglesᐃᐃᐃ
Wavy lineSmooth up and down∿
Dotted lineSeries of dots…
Dashed lineSeries of short lines- - -
Spiral lineCircles going outward◎
Thick lineWide█
Thin lineNarrow─

Emotions of Lines

LineFeelingUse for
HorizontalCalm, peacefulLandscapes, water
VerticalStrength, heightTrees, buildings
DiagonalAction, movementRunning, falling
CurvedSoft, gentleClouds, flowers
ZigzagExcitement, dangerLightning, mountains

Types of Shapes

Shapes are made when lines connect.

Geometric Shapes (Math shapes)

ShapeNameExample object
○CircleBall, sun
□SquareBox, window
△TriangleRoof, mountain
▭RectangleDoor, book
◇DiamondKite

Organic Shapes (Natural shapes)

ShapeDescriptionExample
Leaf shapeIrregular ovalLeaves
Cloud shapeFluffy, softClouds
Flower shapePetals around centerFlowers
Animal shapeIrregularAnimals

Colour Theory

Primary Colors

Primary colors cannot be made by mixing other colors.

ColorMeaning
🔴 RedPrimary
🟡 YellowPrimary
🔵 BluePrimary

Secondary Colors

Secondary colors are made by mixing two primary colors.

MixResult
Red + Yellow🟠 Orange
Yellow + Blue🟢 Green
Blue + Red🟣 Purple (Violet)

Tertiary Colors

Mixing a primary color with a secondary color gives tertiary colors (e.g., red-orange, yellow-green).

The Color Wheel

┌─────────────┐ │ Yellow │ │ 🟡 Y-O │ │ Y-G │ │ │ ┌───┴───┐ ┌───┴───┐ │Orange │ │ Green │ │ 🟠 │ │ 🟢 │ │ R-O │ │ B-G │ └───┬───┘ └───┬───┘ │ │ │ Red─Blue │ │ 🔴 🔵 │ │ R-P P-B │ │ Purple │ │ 🟣 │ └─────────────┘

Warm and Cool Colors

Warm Colors 🔥Cool Colors 💧
RedBlue
OrangeGreen
YellowPurple
Feel exciting, energeticFeel calm, peaceful

Color Mixing Practice

Color 1Color 2Result
RedWhitePink
BlueWhiteLight blue
BlackAny colorDarker shade
YellowRedOrange
BlueYellowGreen

Tint, Shade, and Tone

TermMeaningHow to make
TintColor + WhiteAdd white to make lighter
ShadeColor + BlackAdd black to make darker
ToneColor + GrayAdd gray to make duller

Types of Painting Techniques

TechniqueHow to do itEffect
Flat washEven layer of colorSmooth background
Graded washColor fades from dark to lightSky, sunset
Dry brushLess water on brushRough, textured look
Wet-on-wetPaint on wet paperSoft, blurry edges
PointillismSmall dots of colorBlending by eye
SpongingDab with spongeCloud, leaf texture

Lesson 2: Observation Drawing and Imaginary Drawing

Observation Drawing

Observation drawing means drawing what you actually see in front of you.

Steps for Observation Drawing

StepAction
1Look carefully at the object
2Notice the shape, size, and details
3Draw the basic outline lightly
4Add details gradually
5Shade or color what you see
6Compare your drawing to the real object

Tips for Observation Drawing

TipExplanation
Draw what you SEE, not what you KNOWDo not draw a “perfect” apple – draw THIS apple
Use basic shapes firstCircle for apple, rectangle for table
Look more than you drawSpend 50% time looking
Check proportionsCompare sizes (is the apple bigger than the cup?)
Squint your eyesHelps see values (light and dark)

Easy Objects for Observation Drawing

DifficultyObjects
EasyApple, leaf, pencil, cup
MediumFlower, shoe, book, bottle
HardFace, hand, bicycle, plant

Imaginary Drawing

Imaginary drawing means drawing from your imagination – things you have seen or things you invent.

Ideas for Imaginary Drawing

IdeaWhat to draw
Dream worldA planet you imagine
Fantasy characterMonster, fairy, robot
Future inventionFlying car, robot helper
Animal mixLion + eagle = griffin
Underwater worldMermaids, talking fish
Space adventureAstronaut on new planet

Steps for Imaginary Drawing

StepAction
1Close your eyes and imagine
2Think of a story or idea
3Sketch the main shapes lightly
4Add details and characters
5Outline with darker pencil or pen
6Color your drawing

Lesson 3: Mural Art

What is a Mural?

A mural is a large painting made directly on a wall, ceiling, or other permanent surface.

History of Murals

CultureFamous murals
PrehistoricCave paintings (animals, hands)
EgyptianTomb paintings
RomanFrescoes on walls
MexicanDiego Rivera’s history murals
NepaliMural paintings in temples and monasteries

Types of Murals

TypeDescription
FrescoPainting on wet plaster
GraffitiStreet art (with permission)
MosaicSmall tiles or stones arranged
Painted muralDirectly painted on wall

Themes for School Murals

ThemeIdeas
NatureTrees, animals, rivers, mountains
CultureTraditional dance, festivals, costumes
PatriotismNational flag, heroes, monuments
EducationChildren reading, school, books
EnvironmentSave trees, clean earth, recycling
UnityChildren of different cultures playing

Steps to Create a Mural

StepAction
1Choose a theme
2Plan the design on paper (sketch)
3Get permission for the wall
4Clean the wall surface
5Draw a grid to scale up the design
6Sketch the outline on the wall
7Paint the background first
8Paint larger shapes next
9Add details and outlines last
10Let it dry and seal (if needed)

Materials for Murals

MaterialUse
Wall paint (emulsion, acrylic)Main painting
Large brushesCovering large areas
Small brushesDetails
Chalk or charcoalSketching on wall
Spray paintFor certain effects
Sealer/varnishProtect finished mural

Famous Murals Around the World

MuralLocation
Sistine Chapel ceilingVatican City (Michelangelo)
GuernicaSpain (Picasso)
Detroit Industry MuralsUSA (Diego Rivera)
Thangka paintingsNepal, Tibet (Buddhist art)

💡 Teamwork: Murals are often made by groups. Everyone can help!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The three primary colors are ______, ______, and ______.
  2. Red + Yellow makes ______.
  3. A ______ is a painting on a wall.
  4. Drawing what you actually see is called ______ drawing.
  5. Drawing from your imagination is called ______ drawing.

Exercise 2: What Color Do You Get?

Mix these colors:

  1. Blue + Yellow = ______
  2. Red + Blue = ______
  3. Red + Yellow = ______
  4. White + Red = ______
  5. Black + Blue = ______

Exercise 3: Warm or Cool?

Write “Warm” or “Cool” for each color.

  1. Red ______
  2. Blue ______
  3. Yellow ______
  4. Green ______
  5. Orange ______

Exercise 4: Match the Line to Its Feeling

LineFeeling
Horizontala) Excitement
Zigzagb) Calm
Diagonalc) Strength
Verticald) Movement

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. red, yellow, blue; 2. orange, 3. mural, 4. observation, 5. imaginary

Exercise 2: 1. Green, 2. Purple/Violet, 3. Orange, 4. Pink, 5. Dark blue

Exercise 3: 1. Warm, 2. Cool, 3. Warm, 4. Cool, 5. Warm

Exercise 4: Horizontal→b, Zigzag→a, Diagonal→d, Vertical→c

Great work! Colors and lines are your tools. Let your imagination shine on paper and walls!

🎯
